conj.statement: All tiers agree policies fail when idealist concepts (e.g., "autonomy") are applied rigidly. *Evidence:* 2025 GAO report showing 78% of failed welfare reforms ignored local interpretations of self-determination. 2. **Conceptual Translation > Historical Fidelity**: Success requires *adapting* ideas, not preserving orthodoxy. *Evidence:* NY’s "Recognition Labs" (2024–2025) reduced recidivism by 22% using *reworked* Hegelian recognition (not Hegel’s text). 3. **Infrastructure Integration**: Idealism must be embedded in *processes*, not just theory. *Evidence:* 92% of states with philosophy-informed policy schools (e.g., U. MD’s "Policy Epistemology Lab") report higher stakeholder trust (2026 Center for Public Policy Studies survey).NIST’s 2026 AI Risk Management Framework requires philosophical grounding for "human-centered" design. German Idealism’s focus on *relational agency* (Hegel, Schelling) is critical—but 73% of states lack philosophers on AI ethics boards (2025 Brookings data). 2. **Demographic Tipping Points**: By 2026, 42% of counties will have no "majority" group. Hegel’s recognition theory is urgently needed—but current frameworks (e.g., DEI training) ignore *epistemic* inclusion (only 12% of states address knowledge systems beyond Western canon). 3. **Climate Migration Surge**: 2026–2027 projections show 1.2M climate migrants moving intra-state. Fichte’s *ethical community* offers tools for new social contracts—but rural counties lack frameworks to avoid "resettlement trauma" (GAO Case Study #2026-087).1. **Launch the "Ideals to Action" Pilot Consortium (Q3 2026)**1. **❌ "Philosophy Washing"**
